instructions: Expose local HTTP ports on public HTTPS URLs via ngsrv. Call tunnel.start for a public URL, tunnel.list to see active tunnels, token.provision for a 12-hour agent token.

tools.name: tunnel.start
tools.title: Start tunnel
tools.description: Expose a local HTTP server on a public HTTPS URL via ngsrv. Returns public_url and local_port. Use for webhooks, OAuth callbacks, and sharing dev servers.

port.description: TCP port your local HTTP server listens on. Example: 3000 for Next.js, 8080 for APIs.

tools.name: tunnel.list
tools.title: List tunnels
tools.description: List HTTPS tunnels started by this MCP process. Returns port, public_url, and started_at for each tunnel.

limit.description: Maximum number of tunnels to return. Defaults to all active tunnels.

tools.name: tunnel.stop
tools.title: Stop tunnel
tools.description: Stop an ngsrv tunnel started by this MCP session. Pass the same port given to tunnel.start.

port.description: Local port of the tunnel to stop. Example: 3000.

tools.name: token.provision
tools.title: Provision agent token
tools.description: Obtain a 12-hour ngsrv agent token. Uses NGSRV_API_TOKEN when set; otherwise starts a free device trial.

refresh.description: When true, request a new trial token even if one exists in the environment.

tools.name: docs.install
tools.title: Install help
tools.description: Return ngsrv CLI install commands and MCP client configuration snippets for Cursor and Claude Desktop.

client.description: Target client name for tailored config. Example: cursor, claude.

prompts.name: tunnel.quickstart
prompts.description: Expose a local dev server on a public HTTPS URL.

prompts.name: docs.setup
prompts.description: Show MCP and CLI setup for ngsrv.

resources.name: quickstart
resources.description: Install commands for ngsrv CLI, npx @ngsrv/cli, and @ngsrv/mcp.
